Important:
CAMPAIGN VERIFICATION
Integral Ad Science (“IAS”), a 3rd party verification tool, is used to monitor authenticity (brand-safe, non-fraud, in-geo impression delivery) and viewability on a weekly basis.
Vendor to monitor/optimize on an ongoing basis in order to meet/exceed the guarantees in place for the campaign. Starcom reserves the right to use IAS pre-bid filters on all
programmatically bought inventory for both quality and viewability. For CPM/dCPM campaigns, any impressions that do not meet the campaign guarantees will not be billable to
Starcom/McDs.
FREQUENCY CAPS
Frequency is measured per user, per quarter, per partner. Frequency caps should be strictly observed. Any changes to the frequency cap will require written approval from
Starcom. For all CPM/dCPM buys, any impressions served above the quarterly frequency cap will not be billable to Starcom/McDs.
CAMPAIGN GUARANTEES
1. 96% pass rate imp/click delivery, measured by IAS.
2. 96% quality impressions rate imp/click delivery, measured by IAS.
3. <5% invalid traffic rate imp/click delivery, measured by IAS.
4. 100% in-geo (Continental US+AK/HI only; excludes US Territories) imp/click delivery, measured by IAS.
5. 70% viewability imp/click delivery, measured by IAS.
6. 80% VCR delivery, measured by IAS.
7. Partner must actively make transparent optimizations that will improve their ability to meet delivery and quality guarantees and drive success against the designated KPI.
8. Impressions not served above 3 times per week, 10 times per campaign, per user.
Page 1PACING
A pacing document will be provided at campaign launch. Weekly campaign flight dates and pacing will be indicated in the pacing doc.
1. Spend should not deviate from the pacing doc unless specifically stated/approved by Starcom.
2. Starcom/ McDs will not be liable for ad serving fee coverage of any delivered impressions exceeding 110% of contracted impression volume, as reported by CM. Vendor agrees
to cover all 3rd party ad serving costs for delivered impressions that exceed 110% of the contracted impression amount per campaign and for any delivery that does not adhere to
McDs Guidelines as outlined in this IO and in RFP documents.
BUY GUIDELINES
3rd party reports will be used for determination of campaign compliance according to the following guidelines (“Guidelines”):
1. All media impressions/clicks shall run within agreed upon placements in conjunction with the IO, this includes, only serving impressions to United States IP addresses.
2. Impressions/clicks shall not run against the McDs Block List (provided at campaign launch).
3. Ads shall not be placed on websites that promote, advertise, or contain content that is found on the McDs High Severity List (provided at campaign launch).
4. Any updates to Brand Safety parameters are to be made within 1.5 hours of notification from Starcom.
5. Impressions/clicks passed through daisy chaining is not acceptable without Agency’s prior written approval.
6. Impressions/clicks shall not run on sites that allow ad stacking in page display or serve ads within invisible iframes. Impressions shall run according to the PMX Terms and
Conditions.
7. Vendor should separate McDs ads from other McDs campaigns. No more than 1 McDs ad should appear on any given page.
Failure to quickly resolve any issues outlined above will result in cancellation and/or make-good in the form of cash back or other value equivalent to the miss-delivered media, at
Starcom/ McDs discretion.
TRACKING AND REPORTING REQUIREMENTS
McDs’ preferred ad servers are as follows: CM for desktop, mobile, tablet, Innovid for video. CM will be used for billing purposes and delivery reporting unless approved by Starcom
(i.e. dCPC, dCPM, etc.) prior to purchase. If the Vendor is unable to work with an ad serving partner listed above, Vendor must communicate this to the Starcom team before the
launch of the program to discuss alternatives. Inability to work with selective ad server will result in removal from the plan.
1. For takeover/roadblock executions where multiple McDs ads run on one page simultaneously, Starcom will only count impression delivery for one unit for the entire execution
(typically 300x250).
2. Regardless of serving source (site or server direct), all placements will use ad tracking (impression or 1x1 pixel through CM).
3. Partner agrees to accept and adhere to Starcom/McDs taxonomy displayed on Insertion Orders for both placements and campaigns.
4. Tags:
- CM tags will be used to track impression and click data for delivery and measurement attribution.
- IAS verification tags will be used to track quality impression delivery.
- Placed will be used for sales measurement.
- Kochava will be used for app registration measurement.
- Truste/TrustArc in the event partner doesn’t have an internal OBA Compliance solution.
5. Automated reporting from CM will be provided for delivery monitoring.
Page 26.Vendor agrees to provide reporting of actual media spend to Starcom by Monday of each week for the prior week's activity (through Sunday). Vendor will use these reports as the
basis for invoicing Starcom.

Conditions.

launch of the program to discuss alternatives. Inability to work with selective ad server will result in removal from the plan.

Important:
Content Policies and Restrictions
-Walgreens is committed to supporting quality television programming which represents the highest standards of good taste, fair practice, and objectivity. Connect is dedicated to
working closely with its television partners to ensure that all program schedules with said vendors fully represent the Walgreens program guidelines. Avoid placing Walgreens
brands or messages in environments/contents which contain the following:
1) Programming that violates acceptable societal standards relating to excessive or unwarranted acts of violence, graphic displays of brutality, anti-social behavior, discrimination of
minority or religious groups or gratuitous sexual act
2) Programming that glorifies drug or alcohol consumptions or requires any type of view advisory (e.g.: ‘Due to the graphic nature of this program, viewer discretions advised’
3) Any News programming or programming that takes an extreme political stance
4) Any programming that defames the Walgreens brand
5) Any programming that covers topics such as war coverage, terrorist attacks, national disasters and/or human suffering in a ‘dark’ tone (i.e. 9/11, Covid, Hurricane Katrina,
Holocaust)
6) Programs that denigrate any gender, race, color, creed or minority group
7) Programs that feature a celebrity/personality who is experiencing controversy
8) Programs that feature language that is likely to offend viewers. Obscenity, vulgarity, and profanity are to be avoided
9) Any programming that is branded and heavily sponsored by Walgreens competitors
10) Connect will alert client or use best judgment for purchasing movies that were originally rated "R" when released
11) Any programming that has received negative PR or multiple consumer complaints due to content or characters/actors
